raw_response:
Variant B is significantly more useful for me as the operator of Boska Praga. While both reports identify similar issues, B frames them with a greater sense of urgency and provides a much clearer, more actionable playbook. The concept of a "value vacuum" perfectly captures the problem: it's not just about my base prices, but the lack of surrounding promotions and value signals that my competitors use so effectively. B's specific examples, like Praski Butcher's "Tuesday cheap beer combo" or Bazar's "Bąbelkowe wtorki," are concrete tactics I can evaluate immediately. Most importantly, B's analysis of Praski Butcher's review management—using responses to market other deals—is a sophisticated insight that reframes a chore into a strategic opportunity. A is a solid analysis, but B gives me a direct, multi-pronged plan to defend my market position this week.

{"winner": "B", "supporting_urls": ["https://wolt.com", "https://www.google.com/maps/"]}
